message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Wrist-worn devices can keep track of a person’s daily health status, including those likely to become infected with the SARS-CoV-2 virus. Technological solutions using mobile devices are being developed to predict the time course of COVID-19.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>In this proof-of-concept study, we use heart rate data to detect the first sign of infection in people who have been diagnosed with COVID-19 and to monitor the time-course of the illness.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The heart-rate data were analysed using a multiplicative cascade driven by a Gaussian process. This provides two parameters, mean and standard deviation, which when combined with similar parameters estimated from control series, provide a Health Index.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>For 90% of 31 cases, the Health Index tracked COVID-19 infection with the virus and subsequent recovery. The first-sign of COVID-19 was detected on average nine days before symptoms were reported.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Early detection of COVID-19 may lead to a reduction in the spread of the virus. The Heath Index’s potential use for the early detection of complications arising from Long COVID would be an important innovation.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Application of a Multiplicative Cascade Model to Detect the Early Signs of SARS-CoV-2 Infection Using Heart Rate Data

reference.unstructured: Heath RA , Garnett R 2022 submitted. Application of commercial wearable devices for lifestyle monitoring.
